blocks.content: The same porch it had always been — same light, same steps, same everything.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm not ready for tonight to be over.
She leaned against the post. He stood on the step below her, which put them almost eye level, and she looked at him

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We still have the whole summer.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: No distance. No time zones. Just this.
He leaned forward and kissed her again — softer this time, no fireworks overhead, just the porch light and the sound of the neighborhood asleep and her hand finding the front of his jacket.
When they broke apart she was smiling.
He went down the steps. Halfway down the path he turned.
She laughed. He turned and walked home through the dark, and she stood on the porch and watched him until he was gone.
